behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because the failure boundary is parser output handed to normal hook-input handling (module handoff), not just one pure function. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process wiring is sufficient. Rejected higher level: behavior text says only 'valid JSON payloads ... parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling' with no real process requirement and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real parser + hook input handler + in-memory trace/telemetry sink. Feed valid JSON whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`. Assert parse succeeds, handler continues on success path, and no parse-failure trace is emitted.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this behavior requires parser failure plus trace-record construction/context propagation across components. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because malformed input and trace context can be validated without real subprocess/OS I/O. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about trace record content, not real process lifecycle.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser and trace-recording path together in-process. Provide malformed JSON cases (e.g., truncated object, bad escaping). Assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic context fields (offset/snippet/error message) sufficient to localize the malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Unit wins because behavior text is explicitly the 'local error-policy path' mapping an input error to output state. Winner vs System -> Unit wins because no real OS/network/subprocess is required. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text indicating module handoff requirement, real infra, or AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function/object directly with a representative parse-failure error. Assert it returns handled/no-throw result and sets telemetry-intent marker flags as expected.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins initially because behavior spans process input, parsing, and signaling. Winner vs System -> System wins because behavior text explicitly requires 'real process execution' and malformed stdin handling before process exit (subprocess/OS boundary). Rejected lower level: explicit real-process requirement disqualifies in-process-only tests.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook as a real subprocess, write malformed bytes to stdin, close stdin, and wait for exit. Assert process does not crash unexpectedly, exits with expected handled status, and trace signal is emitted before exit (captured via real trace output channel/file/stdout).

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins initially because this is an end-to-end local pipeline across modules. Winner vs System -> System wins because behavior text says 'real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)', which is real process/I/O plumbing. Rejected lower level: explicit real invocation path disqualifies purely in-process wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin payload and configured real trace sink target used in production. Assert the concrete trace artifact is created and contains `json_parse_failed` semantics expected by downstream diagnostics.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because distinction requires exercising parser + classification/trace attribution path over contrasting payload classes, not just one parsing helper. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because the regression can be caught with in-process module wiring; no real OS/subprocess required by behavior text. Rejected higher level: no real infra requirement and no AI/ML decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a paired regression suite in-process: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings and (b) malformed JSON controls. Run through parser+attribution path and assert only malformed cases produce parse-failure attribution while valid-markdown cases stay on success path.
